header.tagline: A discreet enfilade of chambers — where light is filtered, conversations stay, and every material earns its patina.
header.industry_note: Law-firm profile — closed-office ratio high (partners private, associates 2-to-a-room), dark green + leather + brass palette, heavy acoustic separation DnT,A ≥ 40 dB, client-facing enfilade isolated from work zones.

atmosphere.hero_image_theme: City-of-London law firm interior — dark panelled library, deep green walls, brass reading lamps, Chesterfield-adjacent but contemporary, Douglas fir floor, heavy curtains filtering Gracechurch daylight

materials.name: Dinesen Douglas plank, natural oiled
materials.product_ref: Douglas plank, solid, up to 15 m
materials.sustainability: FSC, natural oil finish, low VOC

materials.name: Interface Composure — Deep Sky
materials.application: Associate offices, paralegal neighbourhoods, boardroom floors

materials.name: Farrow & Ball — Card Room Green, eggshell
Mutina Margarita terrazzo
materials.application: Reception desk front and client-lift lobby

materials.name: Gustafs micro-perforated walnut panels
materials.application: Library bookwalls, boardroom panelling, deposition rooms

materials.name: Lindner Life 80 double-glazed acoustic partition
materials.application: Associate 2-person offices and meeting rooms — confidentiality

materials.name: Création Baumann Hush acoustic curtain
materials.application: Boardroom and deposition room — acoustic + visual seclusion on Gracechurch façade

furniture.application: Shared associate offices and paralegal desks
Formal boardroom (12–14 pax) and deposition room
Informal client lounges — discreet enclave off partner corridor
Confidential client calls — distributed on paralegal floor

planting.strategy: Discrete to moderate tier (2–3% of NIA) — no messy vines in client-facing enfilade. South courtyard façade gets a single hero Kentia per partner corridor; north Gracechurch side uses low-light anchors (ZZ, Aspidistra). One statement planted wall in the reception hall only.

light.strategy: 3000 K throughout partner offices, library and client lounges (warm, leather-flattering); 3000 K with dimming in boardrooms; 3500 K in corridors and paralegal neighbourhoods. Brass task lamps at every partner desk; statement Murano pendant over the main boardroom table.

fixtures.application: Every partner desk, library reading tables
Over boardroom and deposition room tables — signature client-facing gesture
Reception hall stair void — first impression from Gracechurch Street

notes: Industry bias applied: law_firm → wood + leather + dark green + brass. Avoided open-plan tropes, bright primary accents, and exposed ceilings (would read as creative agency).
Flex 0.95 + confidentiality brief → every meeting room glazing spec'd at Lindner Life 80 45 dB Rw, not standard single-glazed.
Hero image to be shot in B&W per mood-board-method for professional-services tier; palette swatches remain in colour.
[TO VERIFY] Farrow & Ball Card Room Green against Altamont & Rees brand deck — tagline 'Where discretion meets light' could also justify Pigeon (softer) if partners prefer a lighter register.
[TO VERIFY] Mutina Margarita exact colourway for reception desk — sample board required before Cat B sign-off.
Wine cellar / tasting kitchen not covered in this board — treat as a sub-atmosphere (oak cask, Dinesen floor, dimmable 2700 K) in a follow-up spread.
